channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Managing Long-Term Sick Leave in the Workplace: Legal and Ethical Considerations
item.description: The article explores the challenges of managing an employee who has been on extended sick leave for over a year. Legally, Australian workplace law protects employees from dismissal due to temporary il...

item.title: Manila Mayor Urges DPWH to Speed Up Beata Bridge Repairs Amid Safety Concerns
item.description: Manila Mayor Isko Moreno has called on the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) to expedite the ongoing repairs on Beata Bridge in Pandacan, which has been causing inconvenience to both resi...

item.title: Western Bulldogs struggle with injuries after heavy defeat to Sydney
item.description: The Western Bulldogs face a mounting injury crisis following an 11-goal loss to the Sydney Swans. Aaron Naughton is set for scans on his neck after a frightening fall, though initial signs suggest no ...

item.title: Expansion of IITs and IIMs Raises Concerns Over India’s Global Research Performance
item.description: India has been steadily expanding its network of premier higher education institutions, particularly the Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs) and Indian Institutes of Management (IIMs). However, des...

item.title: Kim Kardashian and Lewis Hamilton spark dating rumours after Malibu outing
item.description: Kim Kardashian and Formula One champion Lewis Hamilton have once again stirred speculation about a possible romantic relationship after being spotted together in Malibu. The two global celebrities wer...

item.title: Nigerian Navy Ready to Partner with ASELSAN for Local Shipbuilding and Naval Tech Development
item.description: The Nigerian Navy don show say dem dey ready to team up with different organisations to push indigenous shipbuilding capacity and make naval operations better for the country. Chief of Naval Staff, Vi...

item.title: Ahmedabad Teen Murdered After Challenging Attackers on Social Media
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Ahmedabad, Gujarat, where a teenage boy lost his life after engaging with attackers on social media. Reports indicate that the teen had posted a daring message on Instagr...

item.title: Adebayo Adelabu Receives Warm Welcome in Ibadan as He Prepares for 2027 Governorship
item.description: Adebayo Adelabu, former Deputy Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) and recent Minister of Power under President Bola Tinubu, dey receive one big and warm welcome when e land Ibadan, Oyo Stat...

item.title: Akande explains shift in Tinubu’s economic team and Edun’s exit
item.description: Former presidential aide Laolu Akande don talk say President Bola Ahmed Tinubu had already made up him mind to replace former Finance Minister Wale Edun long before the change happen. According to Aka...

item.title: Spring runoff prompts road closures and emergency measures in central Saskatchewan
item.description: Central and east-central Saskatchewan are preparing for a challenging spring flood season as above-average runoff is expected across much of the region. According to the Water Security Agency’s April ...

item.title: Omanyala Seeks Another Sub-10-Second Run at Kip Keino Classic
item.description: Ferdinand Omanyala, Kenya's sprint king, is looking to build on his recent form as he heads into the Absa Kip Keino Classic this Friday at Nyayo National Stadium. Fresh from a sensational 9.98-second ...

item.title: New Zealand’s long-term conservation efforts begin to restore native bird populations
item.description: New Zealand is beginning to see tangible results from an ambitious nationwide effort to protect its unique native bird species, many of which were pushed to the brink of extinction by introduced preda...

item.title: Tesla, IBM, Nestlé and Lululemon in Focus After Mixed Earnings and Guidance
item.description: Wall Street analysts are closely watching Tesla, IBM, Nestlé and Lululemon after the companies released first‑quarter results that highlighted strong sales and profit growth alongside cautious investo...

item.title: P.E.I. Cabinet Minister Sidney MacEwen Steps Down Due to Conflict of Interest Over Lobster Licence
item.description: Sidney MacEwen, a cabinet minister in Prince Edward Island, has resigned from his position on the executive council following a conflict regarding his lobster fishing licence. The issue arose when Fis...

item.title: Two persons, including social media influencer, held in Kerala for alleged possession of MDMA
item.description: Police in Kerala have arrested two persons, including a social media influencer, in connection with the suspected possession of MDMA, a banned synthetic drug. The arrest took place during a special op...

item.title: Maryland Bans Surveillance Pricing, Ending Personalised Price Hikes
item.description: Maryland lawmakers have recently approved a landmark bill banning surveillance pricing, a practice in which retailers adjust prices individually based on a shopper's personal data, purchasing history,...

item.title: Tanzania balances foreign investment push with stricter mining licence enforcement under Dira 2050
item.description: This article discusses how Tanzania is currently sending two strong signals to investors as it advances its Dira 2050 development agenda. On one side, the government is actively inviting foreign capit...

item.title: Teck Resources expects rising costs due to Middle East conflict
item.description: Teck Resources Ltd., based in Vancouver, has warned that the ongoing conflict in the Middle East could lead to increased energy costs at its operations in Chile. The company operates the Quebrada Blan...

item.title: East African Nations Coordinate Preparations for AFCON 2027
item.description: On April 23, 2026, CAF Secretary General Samson Adamu meet top government and football officials from Tanzania, Kenya, and Uganda in Kampala, Uganda to check how preparations for AFCON 2027 dey go. At...

item.title: Toronto police confiscate SMS devices linked to cybercrime activities
item.description: Toronto police recently seized multiple SMS blaster devices as part of an ongoing investigation into cybercrime in Canada. SMS blasters are tools capable of sending massive volumes of text messages au...
